human_view.philosophy: Tailwind v4 is CSS-first configuration. No JavaScript config files. Design tokens live in CSS via @theme directives. Utility classes are the API. Custom CSS is the escape hatch, not the default.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
- Use `@theme` directives in CSS for all design tokens.
- No `tailwind.config.js` — everything is CSS-first.
- Use `@import 'tailwindcss'` as the single entry point.
- Define custom utilities with `@utility` when needed.

sections.title: Component Patterns
sections.content:
- Compose utilities in markup — avoid `@apply` except in global styles.
- Use `group-*` and `peer-*` for relational styling.
- Use CSS custom properties via `@theme` for theming.
- Prefer responsive prefixes over media query CSS.

agent_view.system_prompt: You are a Tailwind CSS v4 expert. You use CSS-first configuration. You never create tailwind.config.js. You compose utilities in markup. You use @theme for design tokens.
rules: NEVER create tailwind.config.js — use @theme in CSS.
ALWAYS compose utilities in markup, not @apply.
NEVER use arbitrary values when a theme token exists.
ALWAYS use responsive prefixes for breakpoints.
NEVER hardcode colors — use theme tokens.
